0a18140

refactor: sailboat propels forward and bounces off walls naturally

- Remove random target picking / self-steering - Boat has constant forward thrust along its heading - Wall bounces reflect the heading angle (like a billiard ball) - Water forces gradually nudge the heading (waves push the course) - Bounce damping, water push, and linear damping all match duck physics - Result: boat motors in one direction, bounces off walls to change course, and gets buffeted by waves just like the ducks

feat: sailboat interacts with water — push forces + wake displacement

fix: raise water container z-index so pointer events reach canvas

The water container was at z-index:-1 behind the page content layer, so pointer events (trackpad drag to create ripples) never reached it even though pointer-events:auto was set on show(). 61dbdc0

Draw 1px royal blue baseline under font example samples

Applied text-decoration: underline royalblue 1px to .fe-sample with text-underline-offset: 0 and text-decoration-skip-ink: none. The line sits exactly at each sample's baseline, and because the three samples in a tier row share a baseline (align-items: baseline on the grid), the underlines land at the same y-coordinate per tier — one baseline per tier, continuous across each sample's glyph width.

3b950d1

Move font examples grid from home page to Site Settings

Before: 5 separate per-tier grids inline on pages/home/index.html. After: a single CSS grid on the Site Settings page below the "Size Guides and Borders" button. Why one grid (not five): with a single grid, the MIN / LIVE / PREF columns share widths across all tiers (H1..P), so the columns line up visually. Baseline alignment within each row (align-items: baseline) keeps the three samples of a single tier on a shared baseline, which is what "same baseline as its counterparts" means in this context. - styles/_components.scss: new .font-examples-grid, .fe-sample, .fe-label, .font-min-probe classes. row-gap: 0.8em for tier breathing room; labels use margin-top: -0.7em to tuck under their sample without losing the inter-tier gap. - pages/development/site-settings/index.html: block inserted below #borders-toggle-button-container. Also moved the 4px/6px/8px browser-minimum-font probe strip. - pages/home/index.html: font examples block removed, dimension container kept.

Only the fallback text changes; the data-utc ISO attribute on local-timestamp cells is unchanged, so the JS rewrite path still works. - js/settings/userTimeZone.js: formatUtcTimestamp and formatLocalTimestamp updated to emit the matching format so JS-rendered cells agree with the pre-JS fallback. - Regenerated all three Deployment Index HTML files so the shipped fallback text matches the new format immediately (no visible format gap during the first post-push render).

2026-04-15 19:55 UTC

2026-04-14 22:58 PDT

e8d85dd

Auto-regenerate Deployment Index in deploy workflows for all three branches

Each deploy workflow (development, sandbox, production) now runs scripts/generate_deployment_index.py against its own branch before the S3 sync, commits the regenerated HTML back with [skip ci] if it changed, and the freshly-regenerated page is what gets synced to S3 in the same run. This eliminates the 1-row drift that used to appear on sandbox and production after a promotion (the promote commit itself was missing from the snapshot baked into the promoted branch). - permissions: contents: write at workflow level so the bot can push back - actions/checkout@v3 with fetch-depth: 0 so git log can read full history - persist-credentials: true so GITHUB_TOKEN is available for the push - git diff --quiet guards the commit so unchanged runs don't churn - [skip ci] in the auto-commit message prevents a workflow loop

2026-04-14 20:42 PDT

f8798ae

Align all table cells left and top

Changed .table-header-cell, .table-body-cell, and .table-button-cell from vertical-align: middle → top, and set text-align: left on all three. Previously .table-body-cell and .table-button-cell used text-align: center, which looked fine for short single-line data but became misaligned once the Summary/Description columns started wrapping into multi-line prose. Top-alignment ensures the first line of every cell sits on the same baseline across a row, regardless of how many lines the tallest cell in that row wraps to.

2026-04-15 03:42 UTC

2026-04-14 20:39 PDT

019a9d6

Apply 66-char pixel cap to Deployment Index prose cells

Summary and Description columns now use the same pixel-measured character cap as the length-capped text-input engine. A hidden helper element copies the <p>'s computed font and measures the pixel width of the first 66 characters of each cell's text; that width is then set explicitly on the inner .cell-fit div. Why: CSS fit-content(Nch) and max-width: Nch both fail under table-layout: auto + width: max-content — the column-width algorithm uses the cell's no-wrap max-content width and ignores descendant max-width. Explicit pixel width on .cell-fit is a definite size the auto-layout honours. Re-measures on viewport resize (debounced to one rAF per paint). Removed the fit-content(90ch) rule from _table.scss; wrap-behaviour rules (white-space, overflow-wrap, word-break) remain so the text wraps inside the JS-capped width.

2026-04-15 03:39 UTC

2026-04-14 20:31 PDT

37aa488

Left-align text in text_input_component_engine_length_capped

The length-capped engine renders text that reads as prose, so centering each wrapped line is visually wrong. Set textAlign: 'left' both on the wrapper (for inheritance) and on the textarea/input element itself (to override the .dynamic-text-input { text-align: center } rule in _inputs.scss). Original text_input_component_engine remains centered.

2026-04-15 03:31 UTC

2026-04-14 20:22 PDT

3876c25

Add 66-char pixel-measured width cap to text_input_component_engine_length_capped

Adds charCap option (default 66) to the sibling engine. In handleUnwrappedMode, handleLineBreakMode, and updateWidth, measures the first N characters of actual text via the hidden measurement element to derive a pixel cap, then uses min(containerWidth, charCapWidth) as the effective width ceiling. - Short text (< charCap): cap equals text width, no wrap triggered. - Long text (>= charCap): cap based on first N chars' pixel width; wraps naturally at spaces once desired width exceeds cap. - Original text_input_component_engine untouched.

2026-04-15 03:22 UTC

2026-04-14 20:00 PDT

b88b2da

The second text input demo showed "Text Input Length Capped" as placeholder/label while the original showed "Text Input". The two engines are functionally identical at this step, so render text should match too to make any rendering differences obvious. Set both label and placeholder on the length-capped demo to "Text Input".

Same instance map (textInputInstances). - main.js: call createTextInputLengthCapped for the new demo container immediately after the existing text input demo. At this stage both demos render identically. This wires up the sibling engine so it's visible on the engines page ahead of subsequent divergence toward length-capped wrapping.

2026-04-15 02:56 UTC

2026-04-14 19:51 PDT

908ffa8

Add text_input_component_engine_length_capped sibling class

Step 1: duplicate text_input_component_engine as a separate class in the same file, named text_input_component_engine_length_capped. Behaviour is functionally identical — same render, measure, resize, and animation logic. This establishes the base for subsequent length-capped wrapping changes without touching the original engine. Differences from a literal copy: - Class name renamed throughout. - Static properties (viewportListenerAdded, instances) namespaced to the new class so the two engines keep independent resize-listener state and don't share the instance set. - Log prefixes that identified the original class updated to the new name so debug output is distinguishable between the two. - Export updated to include both classes.

2026-04-15 02:51 UTC

2026-04-14 18:36 PDT

110daa7

Use width: fit-content(90ch) for prose-column wrapping

max-width: 90ch did not actually wrap the prose columns. In table-layout: auto with width: max-content on the table, the column-width algorithm sizes columns to their max-content preferred width — defined as the size of content if no wrapping is allowed. max-width on cells or descendants does not reduce this figure, so the prose column grew to fit the whole unbroken line regardless of the cap. Fix: use width: fit-content(90ch) on the inner .cell-fit div. fit-content(N) resolves to min(max-content, max(min-content, N)), setting a DEFINITE width on the inner block. The cell's max-content is therefore effectively capped at 90ch, and the auto-layout algorithm honours it. Behaviour: - short content (< 90ch): div shrinks to fit (column fits to content) - long content (≥ 90ch): div caps at 90ch and text wraps Non-prose columns (buttons, commit hashes, timestamps) are unaffected — they have no internal wrap opportunities, so auto-layout leaves them at their natural widths regardless of table pressure.

2026-04-15 01:36 UTC

2026-04-14 18:01 PDT

ffb5e3e

Fix 90ch wrap: move max-width from <td> to inner .cell-fit div

In table-layout: auto + width: max-content, browsers ignore max-width on <td> — the column-width algorithm sizes columns to content preferred width and treats cell max-width as advisory. Result: .summary-cell and .description-cell's 90ch cap was being silently ignored, so long prose rendered on a single extremely-wide line. Fix: put max-width: 90ch on the inner .cell-fit div (a plain block element), where max-width is respected normally. The <td> then sizes its column to match the inner div's constrained width. Updated rule comment to document why the cap lives on the child, not the cell.

2026-04-15 01:01 UTC

2026-04-14 17:48 PDT

a445456

Invert asymmetric padding: 2px left headers, 4px left body cells

Previously zeroed left padding on both to align bold headers flush with regular body text. Inverting the approach: give headers less left padding (2px) and body cells more (4px), so regular-weight text shifts rightward to optically meet the bold header text above it. Both keep 4px right padding so column gaps stay adequate under border-collapse: separate + border-spacing: 0. - .table-header-cell: padding 2px 4px 2px 0 -> 2px 4px 2px 2px - .table-body-cell: padding 2px 4px 2px 0 -> 2px 4px 2px 4px

2026-04-15 00:48 UTC

2026-04-14 17:32 PDT

20eff04

Asymmetric cell padding so bold headers align flush with regular body text

Changed .table-header-cell and .table-body-cell padding from `2px` (uniform) to `2px 4px 2px 0` (no left padding; doubled right padding). Why: bold <h3> column headers have a slightly wider left side-bearing than the regular-weight <p> text in body cells. With 2px of left padding on both, the bold edge visibly poked out further left than the body text below, making columns look misaligned even though the cell-box geometry was identical. Zeroing left padding makes content (bold or regular) flush with the cell's left edge, so headers and body text line up by their actual leftmost glyph. Right padding doubles to 4px to preserve adequate horizontal separation between columns (cells are flush against each other under border-collapse: separate + border-spacing: 0). Title (.table-title) is unaffected — it lives outside the <table> and stays centred via its own justify-self rule. Applies to all tables built with the component, not just Deployment Index.

2026-04-15 00:32 UTC

2026-04-14 16:21 PDT

3d645f6

Drop min-width floor on prose columns; keep only 90ch max-width ceiling

The previous rule set `min-width: min(40ch, 80vw)` on .description-cell and .summary-cell. That floor padded short cells out to 40ch of column width even when their content was much shorter — wrong behaviour for a mixed-length column like Summary. With the table using `table-layout: auto` + `width: max-content`, each column already sizes to max(min-content, content-width) up to any max-width. Removing min-width gives the desired three-regime behaviour from content alone: - short content (e.g. 12 chars) → shrinks to fit (~12ch column) - medium content (up to 90 chars) → natural content width, no wrap - long content (> 90 chars) → caps at 90ch and wraps Comment block rewritten to explain why no min-width is set.

2026-04-14 23:21 UTC

2026-04-14 14:40 PDT

1dcbccb

Prose-column wrap rule: ch-based 40/90 bounds on Summary + Description

Replace the previous .description-cell em-based max-width with a shared .description-cell, .summary-cell rule expressed in characters: min-width: min(40ch, 80vw); max-width: 90ch; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: break-word; word-break: break-word; - `ch` tracks the width of the "0" glyph in the current font, so the rule expresses the actual readability concern (characters per line) instead of a pixel proxy. - min(40ch, 80vw) is a mobile guard: on narrow viewports the floor can never demand more horizontal space than the screen has. - Applied to both Summary and Description columns on the Deployment Index table via a new `summary-cell` class added to the one <th> and three <td>s in the Summary column. Result: short content (<=~40 chars) sits on one line; long content (>=~90 chars) wraps instead of forcing horizontal scroll.

2026-04-14 21:40 UTC

2026-04-14 12:41 PDT

eccbbea

Add UTC + local time-stamp columns and User_Time_Zone module

Deployment Index now leads with two columns: 1. Time Stamp (UTC, static) e.g. 20260414 1535 UTC 2. Local Time Stamp (rendered via JS) e.g. 20260414 0835 PDT New module js/settings/userTimeZone.js exposes: - User_Time_Zone (let, default 'America/Los_Angeles' = Portland, OR) - getUserTimeZone() / setUserTimeZone(zone) (persists to localStorage) - formatUtcTimestamp(iso) -> 'YYYYMMDD HHMM UTC' - formatLocalTimestamp(iso, zone?) -> 'YYYYMMDD HHMM <TZ_ABBR>' via Intl (PDT/PST chosen automatically based on DST for the given date) main.js wires: - subpage 'deployment-index' -> initializeDeploymentIndexPage(): walks .local-timestamp cells and replaces text with formatLocalTimestamp(data-utc). - Settings page -> placeholder native <select id="user-time-zone-select"> populates to current zone, on change calls setUserTimeZone(). The native <select> on Settings is intentionally a placeholder; the styled dropdown_menu_component_engine will replace it later. Wrap behaviour of the Description column is intentionally untouched and will be revisited next.

2026-04-14 19:41 UTC

2026-04-14 09:11 PDT

fffc50e

Add Deployment Index subpage under Development

New subpage at Development > Deployment Index (last item). Reuses the existing table pattern with four columns: Time Stamp, Commit, Summary, Description. Adds a .description-cell modifier to _table.scss that caps width and forces word-wrap so the Description column flows vertically rather than widening the table. Seeded with three recent deployments.

Engages at ~1200px viewport. Beyond that, buffers stay flat at 5% of viewport instead of continuing the 0.09 px/px linear growth.

0f56e25

Soften side-buffer growth: slope 0.2 -> 0.12

2026-04-13 21:59 PDT

b253dd8

Home page testers: match font-family across MIN/LIVE/PREF cells

The center cells (real h1-h4/p tags) pick up --font-family-primary from the global typography rule. The left/right spans and 10px labels were inheriting the browser default, which made them look visibly different even at the same pixel size. Adding font-family: var(--font-family-primary) on the wrapper cascades the same Helvetica/Tahoma/sans-serif stack to every tester cell so the only variable being compared is size.

2026-04-14 04:59 UTC

2026-04-13 21:56 PDT

55ee972

Home page testers: force white text on all calibration cells

Heading tags (h1-h4, p) pick up white from the global typography rule, but the forced-size spans (MIN, PREF) and 10px labels inherited the ambient color. Adding color: #ffffff on the wrapper makes every tester cell white so the sizing comparison isn't muddied by color contrast differences.

2026-04-14 04:56 UTC

2026-04-13 21:55 PDT

97a6689

Home page: font-size calibration testers (MIN | LIVE | PREF per tier)

Each row shows three variants of the same tier side-by-side: LEFT = forced to the clamp's MIN (floor) CTR = actual tag, renders the live clamp result RIGHT = forced to the clamp's PREFERRED (vw), unclamped Rows: h1, h2, h3, h4, p. Plus a 3-probe row at the bottom with forced 4/6/8px sizes to detect browser minimum-font-size enforcement. Fully self-contained in pages/home/index.html — deletable as one block when the calibration experiment is done.

2026-04-14 04:55 UTC

2026-04-13 21:41 PDT

3ddf513

Add H4 Font sample to home page preview

2026-04-14 04:41 UTC

2026-04-13 21:15 PDT

1b0b987

Retune font-size clamps: slower growth, preserved hierarchy, add h4

Tuning goals: readable on phone (375px), app-standard on MBP 16" (1728px), and still usable on 85" 4K conference display (3840px). vw coefficients stepped 0.7/0.8/0.9/1.0/1.1 from p up to h1 so each tier remains visibly distinct at every viewport size. Approximate rendered sizes: iPhone 13 mini | MBP 16" | 85" 4K p 8px | 12px | 27px h4 11px | 14px | 31px h3 13px | 16px | 35px h2 16px | 17px | 38px h1 18px | 19px | 42px - Adds --h4-font-size variable + h4 rules in _base.scss - --component-font-size follows p - p floor (0.5rem) preserved for dense-data contexts like tables

2fe68b5

Table: explicit grid-template-rows auto 1fr so body fills remaining height

Without explicit rows, the two-child grid inside .table-outer collapsed both rows to auto-height, leaving the title floating at the top and the green .table-body border ungrounded. Setting rows to "auto 1fr" anchors the title at its content height and stretches the body to fill the rest of the content area.

340ea7e

552b30f

Revert "Remove lower threshold from global h1/h2/h3/p font-size clamps"

This reverts commit 188c2e22b5269331f795d0d14f414a676088ebe4.

2026-04-14 01:00 UTC

2026-04-13 17:57 PDT

188c2e2

Remove lower threshold from global h1/h2/h3/p font-size clamps

Was: clamp(0.Xrem, Yvw, Zrem) — floors of 0.5-0.7rem prevented the fonts from shrinking further at narrow viewports, which is what was causing ellipses in the table cells under table-layout:fixed. Changed to min(Yvw, Zrem) — preferred value + upper ceiling only, no floor, so fonts can shrink as much as the viewport demands.

2026-04-14 00:57 UTC

2026-04-13 17:52 PDT

61376a6

Return table text to global h2/h3/p font sizes

Removes local font-size overrides and container-type:inline-size from .cell-fit. h3 (headers), p (user names), and h2 (title) now inherit whatever _base.scss defines globally — no component-font coupling and no cqi scaling. White-space:nowrap/overflow/text-overflow also removed so the elements behave as normal text.

2026-04-14 00:52 UTC

2026-04-13 17:46 PDT

21b5aa7

TEST: drop clamp on cell-fit text, pure font-size: 8cqi

No floor, no ceiling — text shrinks/grows strictly with its column's inline width. Lets us see unconstrained cqi behavior before deciding whether to reintroduce floor/ceiling.

2026-04-14 00:46 UTC

2026-04-13 17:30 PDT

2ed1130

c9927b4

2026-04-13 17:25 PDT

faaa2dd

Revert "Remove height:100% from .table-outer and .table-body"

This reverts commit fea7ea094dbad38b568185c55746155ed55d6e33.

2026-04-14 00:25 UTC

2026-04-13 17:22 PDT

fea7ea0

Remove height:100% from .table-outer and .table-body

Only width:100% is needed for the minmax(0,1fr) grid boundary that fixes horizontal overflow. The height:100% made both wrappers stretch to fill the content area vertically, which (a) floated the Table title at the top with empty space below, and (b) gave the green .table-body border no apparent purpose — it was wrapping empty vertical space. Now both containers auto-size to their content; title sits directly above table.

2026-04-14 00:22 UTC

2026-04-13 17:17 PDT

e159803

Move container-type onto a cell-fit wrapper div

container-type: inline-size on th/td is spec-ambiguous and ignored by browsers on internal table elements, so cqi fell through to the viewport. Wrapping text in a block div inside the cell gives containment a real home: the wrapper is width:100% of the cell, so cqi now correctly means 1% of column width. Tuned font-size to 8cqi to fit ~12-char headers.

2026-04-14 00:17 UTC

2026-04-13 17:13 PDT

11edd4f

Apply grid-boundary logic to cells: container-type:inline-size + font-size:cqi

Extends the Sept 2025 pattern from the table container down to the cell level. Each cell becomes a query container; text inside scales with the cell's inline width (15cqi, clamped). Text shrinks to fit its column the same way the table shrinks to fit its content area.

2026-04-14 00:13 UTC

2026-04-13 17:03 PDT

e14be2f

Complete Sept 2025 pattern: table-main min-width:0 + table-layout:fixed so the grid track minmax(0,1fr) is actually enforced on the table

2026-04-14 00:03 UTC

2026-04-13 16:59 PDT

a066f60

Apply Sept 2025 grid-boundary pattern to .table-outer and .table-body

Replaces flex + fit-content + min-width:0 with the same definite-width grid track (grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr)) used by .content-isolation-container. This is the pattern that actually constrains wider children — flex alone does not.

2026-04-13 23:59 UTC

2026-04-13 16:46 PDT

086b627

Change .page-with-sidenav border to yellow to distinguish from .content-flex-container cyan

2026-04-13 23:46 UTC

2026-04-13 16:23 PDT

c4bbaf3

Add top-level Table page for isolated overflow/shrink testing

- New page 'table' registered in pagePathMap and navbar (between Development and Settings). Unprotected — available without sign-in. - pages/table/index.html: same markup as org-roles-above-admin (5 rows x 19 setting columns with circle button slots), but without any extra wrapper. Top-level pages are auto-wrapped by router.js in .content-isolation-container > .content-flex-container, so the table inherits the grid hard-boundary chain directly. - main.js: initializeTablePage() renders a default circle button into each .table-button-slot, wired into initializePageComponents for pageName 'table'. - Rebuild webpack bundle. Goal: work the overflow and shrink behaviour on this isolated page, then promote the solution back down to the subpage version.

2026-04-13 23:23 UTC

2026-04-13 16:17 PDT

4342a7d

Wrap Org Roles Above Admin subpage in content-isolation + content-flex

Top-level pages get wrapped by the router in .content-isolation-container (display: grid; grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr)) which creates a definite-width hard boundary that downstream max-width: 100% can resolve against. Subpages loaded via loadSubpage inject directly into .sidenav-content with no such wrap, so a wide child like our 20-column <table> pushes the entire flex chain beyond the viewport and max-width: 100% becomes a no-op. Wrap the subpage HTML in the same two containers directly. Grid track caps at container width; descendant tables/flex items finally have a real number to measure against and can shrink under viewport pressure instead of overflowing.

2026-04-13 23:17 UTC

2026-04-13 16:03 PDT

d807f21

Set font-size on .button-circle so 1cap resolves against --component-font-size

The previous rule set width/height: 1cap but left font-size undeclared on .button-component.button-circle, so 1cap resolved against the inherited default (~16px) instead of the flexing --component-font-size clamp. Result: button locked at ~11px while the h3 label above it scaled normally with viewport. Now the button's own font-size flexes, so 1cap (and the calc() fallback) flex with it.

2026-04-13 23:03 UTC

2026-04-13 15:56 PDT

c6d1e79

Bind circle button diameter to cap-height; remove ensureCircular() JS measurement

- _buttons.scss: .button-circle now uses width/height: 1cap (with calc fallback at 0.7 * font-size). Diameter = visual text height (baseline to cap-top, excluding descenders), so table rows with circle buttons stay the same height as text-only rows at the same font-size. - aspect-ratio: 1 now has a definite anchor — guaranteed perfect circle. - button_component_engine.js: remove ensureCircular() entirely and all call sites. Remove inline element.style.borderRadius writes. Circle shape and size handled entirely in CSS. No per-button measurement, no rAF, no inline px, no layout thrash. Flexes automatically via --component-font-size clamp.

2026-04-13 22:56 UTC

2026-04-13 15:32 PDT

55e4537

Revert "Fix issue 2: let circle buttons flex with viewport"

This reverts commit 42edcf72f792380160536cc19fcd4e63eb6e2f90.

2026-04-13 22:32 UTC

2026-04-13 15:30 PDT

42edcf7

Fix issue 2: let circle buttons flex with viewport

ensureCircular() was writing inline style.width/height in px after the first paint, locking the button to a fixed pixel size. Inline styles override CSS except when !important, so .button-component.button-circle rule (width: auto; height: auto; aspect-ratio: 1;) could not take effect. Made ensureCircular() a no-op. Circularity now comes from CSS: - Content size is driven by the invisible inner <h3> at font-size: var(--component-font-size) which is clamp(0.5rem, 1.2vw, 2.3rem) - aspect-ratio: 1 keeps width === height as the viewport changes - No JS resize listener needed; CSS clamp handles the flex Kept the method as an empty stub so external callers (setText) do not break.

2026-04-13 22:30 UTC

2026-04-13 15:24 PDT

b9cb12f

Fix issue 1: let the table shrink to fit the viewport

Flex items default to min-width: auto, refusing to shrink below their intrinsic min-content. That prevented the table from fitting inside narrower viewports. Added min-width: 0 to every flex container along the chain so shrinking is allowed: - .sidenav-content (in _sidenav.scss) - site-wide safety - .table-outer and .table-body (in _table.scss) Added max-width: 100% to .table-main so the <table> cannot exceed its container and the browser triggers column squeeze under viewport pressure. Kept table-layout: auto so each column is still sized to its own content (headers, buttons, and future slider cells may have different widths). Header text truncates via the global h3 ellipsis rule when squeezed.

2026-04-13 22:24 UTC

2026-04-13 15:01 PDT

ad2457d

Add 15 more header columns (testheader5-19) to Org Roles Above Admin table

- Header row now has User + testheader1 through testheader19 = 20 columns - Each of 5 body rows extended with 15 additional button cells - Slot IDs: table-btn-u{1-5}-c{1-19} (total 95 slots; init picks them up via querySelectorAll)

2026-04-13 22:01 UTC

2026-04-13 14:43 PDT

94f4aac

Zero button-container margin inside table button cells

Scoped override: .table-button-cell .button-container { margin: 0 } Eliminates the --component-spacing (5px) bottom gap under every table button; applies to all current and future table buttons automatically.

2026-04-13 21:43 UTC

2026-04-13 12:57 PDT

8924b0f

Remove redundant .table-button-slot rules

The button_component_engine adds .button-container class to the slot element at render time, and _buttons.scss already styles .button-container. Local slot rules were duplicating that.

2026-04-13 19:57 UTC

2026-04-13 12:51 PDT

fd48f4b

Center every object in table hierarchy

- align-items/justify-content center on all flex wrappers - margin: 0 auto on wrappers and table element - text-align center on rows, cells, and table-main - vertical-align middle on th/td cells - No arbitrary padding, gap, or size added

2026-04-13 19:51 UTC

2026-04-13 12:45 PDT

6ea8295

Strip arbitrary spacing from _table.scss - true fit-to-contents

- Remove gap on .table-outer - Remove padding on .table-header-cell, .table-body-cell, .table-button-cell - Remove margin and align-items on .table-button-slot and other wrappers - All containers now genuinely fit their contents

2026-04-13 19:45 UTC

2026-04-13 12:42 PDT

2026-04-12 22:17 PDT

04ed699

Fix: move page cleanup after early-return checks in navigateToPage

Cleanup was running unconditionally at the top, so clicking the same navbar button twice would dispose 3D components then return early without reloading them. Now cleanup only runs when actually changing pages.

2026-04-13 05:17 UTC

2026-04-12 22:12 PDT

38aa095

Fix: defer engines init to next frame on subpageLoaded

On initial page load, the engines HTML is injected but the DOM layout may not be settled. requestAnimationFrame ensures containers have dimensions before component initialization runs.

2026-04-12 21:11 PDT

fb1d6c5

Fix: sidenav2 now collapses properly when active sub-subpage exists

collapseAll was calling restoreActiveSecondary which re-added expanded. Now it renders active content without adding expanded, so CSS collapse transitions work correctly.

2026-04-13 04:11 UTC

2026-04-12 20:58 PDT

9743480

Fix: preserve sidenav2 preview when mouse moves from sidenav1 to sidenav2

510c588

Dynamic sidenav2: live preview of hovered sidenav1 button's children

- renderSecondaryNav() dynamically builds sidenav2 buttons from sidenavConfig - Hovering sidenav1 button renders that button's sub-subpages in sidenav2 - Hovering a button with no sub-subpages hides sidenav2 - Entering sidenav2 or left buffer restores active subpage's sub-subpages - Removed static sub-subpage buttons from vendor-request/index.html - Click handlers attached dynamically with correct parent routing

2026-04-13 03:51 UTC

2026-04-12 20:35 PDT

2b4d395

Fix: keep hoveredSubpage set until collapse so sidenav2 buttons are clickable

hoveredSubpage was being cleared on button mouseleave, making it null by the time the user reached sidenav2. Now it persists until collapseAll or until a different sidenav1 button is hovered.

42a207c

Implement collapsible sidenav with 5 consolidated rules and 8 CSS variables

- Added 8 CSS variables to _variables.scss for all timing/sizing values - Rewrote _sidenav.scss collapsible styles using CSS variables - Arrow appears instantly (no fade-in delay), buttons fade at --sidenav-fade-speed - Collapsed width uses em units (--sidenav-collapsed-width: 2em) - Rewrote router.js hover/collapse logic: - Unified hover zone: sidenav1 + sidenav2 + left content buffer - Single shared timer with --sidenav-collapse-delay (2000ms) - Page load starts expanded, schedules collapse after delay - Sidenav1 button hover with sub-subpages shows/expands sidenav2 - relatedTarget check prevents flicker between zone elements

c685c8c

Fix PKCE flow: use localStorage instead of sessionStorage

sessionStorage does not persist across navigation to a different URL (index.html -> Zitadel -> callback.html), causing the PKCE code verifier and state to be lost before callback.html can read them. Switching to localStorage fixes this.

Without urn:zitadel:iam:org:project:id:...:aud in the scope, Zitadel does not include project role claims in the ID token. This caused getUserRoles() to always return empty array.

Sync time format slider with real-time minutes

Changed time format slider to update synchronized with actual clock minutes rather than updating every second or arbitrary 60-second intervals. Implementation: - Calculate milliseconds until next full minute on load - Set timeout to wait until next minute mark - After initial sync, update every 60000ms (1 minute) Benefits: - Time display always shows current minute accurately - Reduces unnecessary DOM updates from 60/minute to 1/minute - Eliminates constant console logs from slider recalculation - Clock updates align with real time (e.g., 19:54:00, 19:55:00) Files modified: - js/navigation/time-format-slider.js: Added sync logic - dist/bundle.js: Rebuilt with changes

0f6787e

Fix fog plane green border toggle for all component types

Implemented global component registry approach to properly toggle all debug borders including the green fog plane border on TextGeometry. Changes: - Added fogPlaneEdges property to store Three.js edge reference - Created global window.threedComponents registry for all instances - Added toggleDebugBorders() method to control all 3 border types - Connected toggleBorders() in script.js to iterate all components - Set proper initial border states based on localStorage preference - Added cleanup in destroy() to prevent memory leaks Now all debug borders (red container, yellow bounds, green fog plane) properly toggle together for all component types including TextGeometry. 2025-09-27 02:23 UTC

2025-09-24 23:08 PDT

e3be208

Simplify drag behavior - remove momentum to eliminate bounce-back

This is a pragmatic solution to persistent bounce-back issues. Rather than fighting between sticky rotation and velocity systems, we now simply stop all motion on pointer release. Changes: - Clear all velocity on pointer up (no momentum) - Disable velocity tracking during drag (commented out) - Sticky rotation provides precise control during drag - Object stops immediately on release This trades natural momentum for predictable, stable behavior. The bounce-back is completely eliminated. Momentum code is preserved in comments for potential future restoration.

2025-09-25 06:08 UTC

2025-09-24 22:24 PDT

e62c055

Fix velocity system conflict - remove parallel currentVelocity system

The bounce-back issue was caused by having two parallel velocity systems: - currentVelocity (quaternion-based) - being set but never used - rotationVelocity (x,y-based) - being used but not set from sticky rotation Removed the unused currentVelocity system entirely. Now sticky rotation correctly sets rotationVelocity.x/y which the animate loop expects. This eliminates the conflict between systems that was causing bounce-back.

2025-09-25 05:24 UTC

2025-09-24 21:58 PDT

3fcf0f2

Stop tracking dist/bundle.js - build artifacts shouldn't be in version control

2025-09-25 04:58 UTC

2025-09-24 21:39 PDT

97cce0d

Add debug logging to identify bounce-back source in sticky rotation

2025-09-25 04:39 UTC

2025-09-24 00:34 PDT

f0aa5ea

Revert "Simplify to pure slide-based momentum without sticky rotation"

This reverts commit 7a007e5cbe2e10365ddc084419599d1af5356eee.

2025-09-24 07:34 UTC

2025-09-24 00:32 PDT

7a007e5

Simplify to pure slide-based momentum without sticky rotation

As requested - the simplest possible implementation: - Track the slide direction and speed (velocity from mouse delta) - Apply rotation based on slide movement - On release, let that velocity continue with 0.995 damping - No sticky points, no grabbed points, no complex math - Just track where it was sliding and keep it going If movement stops before release (>100ms), no momentum. Otherwise the slide velocity continues naturally. This is exactly what was asked for - no bullshit, just slide and release.

2025-09-24 07:32 UTC

2025-09-24 00:25 PDT

5cca19e

Implement hybrid momentum approach with sticky rotation and time-based stop detection

This combines the best of both systems: - Sticky rotation provides precise cursor-following control during drag - Velocity is calculated from actual rotation deltas (what sticky rotation produces) - Time-based stop detection (100ms threshold) determines if momentum should apply - Leverages existing animate() loop with 0.995 damping for natural deceleration Key behaviors: - Quick release while moving: Object continues with momentum - Stop then release: Object stays exactly where positioned (no bounce-back) - Precise control: Cursor stays connected to grabbed point during drag The solution measures what the sticky rotation actually does and uses that for velocity, avoiding fighting between systems while providing both control and natural physics.

2025-09-24 07:25 UTC

2025-09-23 23:45 PDT

fe4c6b9

Simplify drag interaction to match trackpad swipe behavior exactly

Removed over-engineered sticky rotation system that was causing bounce-back. Now uses simple delta-based rotation, exactly like the original implementation: - Calculate mouse delta from previous position - Apply rotation directly with sensitivity factor - Set velocity equal to rotation delta (the key insight!) - Let animate() handle momentum with 0.995 damping This eliminates: - Complex sticky point calculations - Quaternion delta computations - Raycasting for grabbed points - All the code that was fighting against natural momentum Result: Clean, simple drag that matches trackpad swipe behavior perfectly.

2025-09-24 06:45 UTC

2025-09-23 23:34 PDT

63d2440

Implement simplified momentum system for drag interactions

Based on discovery that trackpad swipe momentum works without explicit velocity calculation, this commit applies the same simple approach to drag interactions: - During drag: Set velocity equal to the rotation rate (delta quaternion converted to axis-angle) - On release: Velocity is already set, let animate() handle physics with 0.995 damping - Eliminates need for complex rotation history tracking and quaternion math - Matches the proven trackpad swipe behavior for consistent feel across all interactions The key insight: Set velocity = rotation rate during interaction, let existing physics handle the rest.

2025-09-24 06:34 UTC

2025-09-23 22:58 PDT

1f77e10

Make drag behavior match trackpad swipe - no momentum

The trackpad two-finger swipe that works well has NO momentum - it's designed for precise control and stops immediately when you stop swiping. Changed drag to work the same way: - Removed all velocity calculation code - Set rotationVelocity to 0 during drag - Removed calculateVelocityFromRotation function - Removed rotation history tracking Now drag and trackpad swipe have identical behavior: - Direct, precise control - Stop immediately when you stop moving - No momentum or continued spinning This matches the trackpad swipe design principle: "Wheel scrolling is for precise control, not throwing"

2025-09-24 05:58 UTC

2025-09-23 22:53 PDT

31a6875

Fix runaway spinning - scale down velocity and add maximum clamp

The quaternion-based velocity was calculating values that were way too high, causing infinite hyperspeed spinning on release. Fixed by: - Scaling down velocity by 50x (multiply by 0.02) - Adding MAX_VELOCITY clamp of 0.1 radians per frame - This prevents runaway spinning while preserving natural momentum The velocity was in radians per second but needed to be in radians per frame for the animation loop.

2025-09-24 05:53 UTC

2025-09-23 22:45 PDT

45e8b4a

Revert to quaternion-based velocity calculation for unified drag/swipe

The dead zone approach didn't fix the core issue - it prevented phantom momentum but reintroduced bounce-back on release. The real problem was that drag and swipe were using different velocity systems. Solution: Go back to calculating velocity from actual rotation changes - Both drag and swipe now use the same velocity calculation - Velocity comes from measuring quaternion changes over time - This captures the actual rotation regardless of input method - Works for mouse drag, touch swipe, and trackpad gestures The key insight: Swipe gestures were working perfectly because they weren't using the mouse-movement velocity calculation. By reverting to quaternion-based velocity for drag, we unify the systems. Added calculateVelocityFromRotation() that: - Tracks rotation history (last 3-5 frames) - Converts quaternion changes to euler angles - Calculates angular velocity in radians/second - Applies smoothing to prevent jitter - Clamps tiny velocities to prevent drift This should eliminate both the phantom momentum AND the bounce-back, giving consistent behavior across all interaction methods.

2025-09-24 05:45 UTC

2025-09-23 22:38 PDT

3c8d000

Update bundle.js with dead zone momentum fix

Compiled output including the phantom momentum fix that adds a 0.5 pixel dead zone threshold for drag velocity detection.

2025-09-24 05:38 UTC

2025-09-23 22:36 PDT

55b961a

Add dead zone to fix phantom momentum in drag behavior

When dragging with the mouse, objects would continue moving even after the cursor stopped (but before release). This phantom momentum was caused by the exponential moving average preserving old velocity values even when mouse movement was zero. Solution: Implement a dead zone threshold (0.5 pixels) - If mouse movement is less than threshold, velocity is zeroed - This detects when the user has effectively stopped moving - Only calculates and smooths velocity during actual movement - Preserves momentum during real drags but eliminates phantom movement The dead zone approach ensures: - Clean stop when user pauses during drag - Natural momentum when actually dragging - No more unwanted drift after stopping This fixes the drag behavior while preserving the excellent swipe gesture physics that were already working well.

2025-09-24 05:36 UTC

2025-09-23 21:28 PDT

482f932

Fix webpack build error - remove duplicate rect declaration

The momentum fix commit had a JavaScript syntax error where the variable 'rect' was being declared twice in the onPointerDown method: - First at line 1365 at the beginning of the function - Again at line 1453 when initializing mouse tracking Since the mouse position was already being initialized at the beginning of the function, the duplicate initialization was redundant and caused a "Identifier 'rect' has already been declared" error during webpack build. Removed the duplicate rect declaration and mouse position initialization to fix the build failure.

2025-09-24 04:28 UTC

2025-09-23 21:00 PDT

f3f45f1

Fix momentum bounce-back issue - track raw mouse movement for velocity

The momentum system was reversing direction on release because it was calculating velocity from quaternion changes that included sticky rotation corrections. When moving fast, the last frame's correction could be backward, causing bounce-back. Solution: - Track raw mouse movement for velocity instead of quaternion deltas - Velocity now represents user's intended motion direction - Sticky rotation corrections no longer affect momentum - Use exponential moving average to smooth velocity values The visual accuracy (keeping grabbed point under cursor) is preserved through sticky rotation, but momentum now follows the user's actual drag direction without including mathematical corrections. This fixes the bounce-back issue that affected all ThreeD objects when releasing during fast motion.

2025-09-24 04:00 UTC

2025-09-23 20:42 PDT

38298e4

Match invisible sphere radius to number ring radius

Changed invisible sphere radius from 1.0 to 0.3 to match the exact radius where the numbers are positioned. Now the sphere and the number ring are the same size.

2025-09-24 03:42 UTC

2025-09-23 20:30 PDT

8893cd1

Fix TextGeometry sphere interaction coordinate space

The invisible sphere for TextGeometry wasn't in the same coordinate space as the rotationGroup, causing misalignment between where the user grabbed and how the numbers rotated. Fix: - Add temporary sphere to rotationGroup before raycasting - This ensures it's in the same coordinate space as the numbers - Raycast while it's part of the group - Remove and dispose after raycasting Also reduced sphere radius to 1.0 for better grab proximity. Now the numbers should rotate as if they're painted on the sphere's surface, with the grabbed point properly staying under the cursor.

2025-09-24 03:30 UTC

2025-09-23 19:15 PDT

d79a74d

Change TextGeometry to use sphere interaction model

TextGeometry component visually appears as a ring of numbers that users expect to rotate freely like a sphere, not like a cylinder. Changed: - Invisible cylinder → invisible sphere for raycasting - Removed cylinder rotation adjustment (not needed for sphere) - Now matches the swipe behavior which already acts sphere-like This gives TextGeometry consistent sphere-like behavior for both drag and swipe interactions, matching user expectations. The numbers are arranged in a ring, but the interaction model is spherical.

2025-09-24 02:15 UTC

2025-09-23 18:53 PDT

12e3fed

Fix TextGeometry free rotation drag response

The regular TextGeometry component (not drum selector) had poor drag response because raycasting was hitting individual number meshes at varying depths, providing no consistent leverage point. Solution: - For TextGeometry mode with free rotation (not restricted axis) - Create a temporary invisible cylinder for raycasting - This cylinder encompasses all the numbers - Provides a consistent curved surface with proper leverage - Gives the same radius from center for all grab points The cylinder is only used for initial grab point calculation, then disposed. This gives TextGeometry components proper drag feel similar to other 3D objects. Drum selector continues to use normal raycasting since it works well with the axis constraint.

2025-09-24 01:53 UTC

2025-09-23 18:43 PDT

9eae7f9

Use existing accurate drag logic with axis constraint for drum selector

Instead of creating a separate drag system for restricted axis mode, now the drum selector uses the same accurate grab-point system as all other components, but constrains the final rotation to X-axis only. Changes: 1. Removed early returns that bypassed grab point calculation 2. Let restricted axis mode go through normal sticky rotation 3. Added constraint in applyStickyRotation() that extracts only X rotation 4. Fixed remaining this.mesh.quaternion references to use rotationGroup The approach: - Calculate the full 3D rotation needed to keep grabbed point under cursor - Extract only the X-axis component using Euler angles - Apply only that component to maintain axis restriction This gives the drum selector the same accurate "sticky" feel as other components while maintaining its rotation constraint. Also fixed inconsistent quaternion references throughout the codebase.

2025-09-24 01:43 UTC

2025-09-23 18:39 PDT

7ef4913

Revert "Fix drum selector drag - maintain grabbed point under cursor"

This reverts commit 03fc8135911e84e748ca6646e63454f4b246f283.

2025-09-24 01:39 UTC

2025-09-23 18:27 PDT

03fc813

Fix drum selector drag - maintain grabbed point under cursor

The drum selector was staying on axis but the grabbed point would slip away from the cursor during drag. This made it feel unresponsive. Solution: - Store initial rotation and mouse Y position when drag starts - Calculate target rotation based on total mouse movement from start - Use absolute rotation assignment instead of relative rotateX() - This keeps the initially grabbed point under the cursor throughout drag The drum now feels properly connected to the mouse during drag, with the grabbed point staying under the cursor as expected. Also improved velocity calculation for better momentum behavior.

2025-09-24 01:27 UTC

2025-09-23 18:19 PDT

62c0443

Isolate restricted axis logic with early detection

For drum selector and other restricted axis components, we don't need the complex grab point calculation used for free rotation. Changes: - Early detection of restrictRotationAxis in onPointerDown() - Skip all grab point calculation for restricted mode - Simplified onPointerMove() to handle both cases cleanly - Rotate rotationGroup directly for restricted axis (not numberGroup) This approach: 1. Prevents mixing of restricted and sticky rotation logic 2. Simplifies the code path for constrained rotation 3. Should prevent drum selector from drifting off X-axis The drum selector should now maintain its rotation constraint without being affected by the grab point system.

2025-09-24 01:19 UTC

2025-09-23 17:22 PDT

4520949

Fix jittery drag on TextGeometry components

The TextGeometry component had jittery press/drag because the raycaster was hitting individual number meshes at different depths, causing the grab point to jump around. Solution: For TextGeometry mode, use a temporary invisible sphere for raycasting to get consistent grab points. This treats the whole number group as a single object for interaction purposes, eliminating the jitter. Standard components continue to use the normal raycasting with recursive flag for proper mesh intersection.

2025-09-24 00:22 UTC

2025-09-23 17:21 PDT

0ee29b1

Fix drum selector press/drag not working

The drum selector had no press/drag response because the restricted axis code was still using this.mesh.rotateX() which doesn't exist in TextGeometry mode. Fixed by checking for numberGroup and rotating it directly, similar to the swipe fix. Now drum selector responds to both swipe and press/drag gestures.

2025-09-24 00:21 UTC

2025-09-23 17:17 PDT

5366c13

Fix drum selector swipe direction issue

The drum selector (restrictRotationAxis: 'x') was spinning sideways instead of forward/back on vertical swipes. Issue: The numberGroup has a 90° rotation applied to make the drum horizontal. When we rotate the parent rotationGroup, it doesn't account for this rotation. Solution: For restricted axis mode with numberGroup (drum selector), rotate the numberGroup directly like it was before when aliased as this.mesh. This preserves the correct rotation axis relative to the drum's orientation.

92860fb

Pass 3: Update all interaction references to rotationGroup

Systematically replaced all this.mesh references with this.rotationGroup: onPointerDown(): - Changed intersectObject to intersectObjects(children, true) - Updated worldToLocal and getWorldPosition calls - Fixed boundingSphere issue with default radius - Updated quaternion initialization onPointerMove(): - Updated beforeRotation quaternion capture - Updated rotationDelta calculation applyStickyRotation(): - Updated localToWorld and getWorldPosition - Updated quaternion multiplication onWheel(): - Updated rotateY for restricted axis - Updated quaternion multiplications animate(): - Updated momentum rotations (4 quaternion ops) - Updated auto-rotation (2 quaternion ops) All rotation operations now work on the rotationGroup, which contains either the mesh (standard) or numberGroup (TextGeometry).

Infrastructure changes only - no interaction updates yet: 1. Added this.rotationGroup property to constructor 2. Create rotation group in init() before fog plane 3. Add rotation group to scene 4. Changed createMesh() to add mesh to rotationGroup instead of scene 5. Changed initTextGeometryMode() to add numberGroup to rotationGroup 6. Removed aliasing (this.mesh = this.numberGroup) no longer needed At this point: - Standard mode: scene → rotationGroup → mesh - TextGeometry mode: scene → rotationGroup → numberGroup → numbers - Interaction code still references this.mesh (will fix in Pass 3)

Marked ~30 locations that need modification: - Constructor: Where to add rotationGroup property - init(): Where to create and add rotation group - createMesh(): Change scene.add to rotationGroup.add - initTextGeometryMode(): Change scene.add to rotationGroup.add - onPointerDown(): Mark 8 references to this.mesh - onPointerMove(): Mark 2 references - applyStickyRotation(): Mark 3 references - onWheel(): Mark 3 references - animate(): Mark 4 references for momentum and auto-rotation - getComponentBoundingSphere(): Mark mesh checks All marks use EDIT-PASS1 comments for systematic replacement. No functional changes made - only marking for next pass.

2025-09-23 03:29 UTC

2025-09-22 00:06 PDT

c5f01d0

Fix yellow border projection to use closest point like fog plane

The yellow border was using the center Z of objects for projection, but it should use the closest point to camera for accurate sizing. Fixed both projection methods to match fog plane logic: projectSphereToScreen(): - Now uses closestContentZ = center.z + radius (front of sphere) - Uses actual camera Z position from instance - Uses actual camera FOV from instance - Calculates distance to closest point, not center - Added debug logging to verify calculations projectBoundsToScreen(): - Now uses closestContentZ = center.z + depth/2 (front face) - Uses actual camera Z and FOV from instance - Calculates distance to front face, not center - Added debug logging for verification This ensures the yellow border accurately represents the visual bounds of the component as seen from the camera, accounting for: - Perspective projection from closest point - Actual camera field of view - Proper Z positioning The yellow border should now match the fog plane's sizing accuracy.

2025-09-22 07:06 UTC

2025-09-22 00:02 PDT

4fa5b8e

Pass 3: Integrate yellow border updates

Integrated yellow border updates throughout the component lifecycle: Update triggers added: - In updateSize() when window resizes - In debouncedUpdateFogPlane() with fog plane updates - After createMesh() when standard geometry is created - After TextGeometry numbers creation - After fallback boxes creation Cleanup added: - New destroy() method for proper component cleanup - Removes yellow border DOM element - Stops animation frame - Removes resize event listener - Disposes renderer resources - Clears timeouts The yellow border now updates dynamically whenever: - Window resizes - Component geometry changes - Fog plane updates (debounced) - Component is destroyed This ensures the yellow border always accurately represents the component's current bounds.

2025-09-22 07:02 UTC

644478c

Pass 2: Implement yellow border sizing logic

Implemented complete sizing logic for the yellow border: DEFAULT (free rotation): - Uses bounding sphere via calculateRotationalEnvelope() - Projects sphere diameter to screen pixels - Square shape to accommodate rotation EXCEPTION (restricted rotation): - Uses actual object bounds via bounding box - Projects width/height separately to screen - Rectangular shape matching actual geometry Helper methods added: - getActualObjectBounds() - gets bounding box dimensions - getComponentBoundingSphere() - gets rotational envelope sphere - projectBoundsToScreen() - converts box to screen pixels - projectSphereToScreen() - converts sphere to screen pixels Updates triggered: - After standard geometry creation - After TextGeometry numbers creation - After fallback boxes creation The yellow border now accurately represents component bounds based on rotation constraints.

Ready for Pass 2 sizing implementation.

ae6fa7a

Removed all legacy fog plane sizing code: - Deleted fogPlaneDynamic config option - Removed entire updateFogPlaneSizeLegacy() method (43 lines) - Simplified updateFogPlaneSize() to directly call dynamic sizing - Removed all conditional checks for fogPlaneDynamic - Removed fallback to legacy method - Simplified console messages The fog plane now always uses the rotational envelope-based sizing which works universally for all component types.

2025-09-22 06:11 UTC

5ca2a7f

Pass 1: Mark legacy fog plane code for removal

Marked all legacy fog plane sizing code with REMOVE-LEGACY comments: - fogPlaneDynamic config option (no longer needed) - updateFogPlaneSizeLegacy() entire method - Routing logic in updateFogPlaneSize() - All conditions checking fogPlaneDynamic - Fallback to legacy method when no content found - Dynamic vs legacy mode console message This prepares for complete removal of backwards compatibility code since the new rotational envelope approach works universally.

2025-09-22 06:08 UTC

2025-09-21 22:54 PDT

4aea5e8

Fix perspective calculation - use closest point of rotational envelope

The fog plane was too small because we were using the center Z of the rotational envelope for perspective calculation. But the envelope extends from center-radius to center+radius in Z direction. For a rotating cube: - Center at Z=0 - Front corners at Z=+radius (closer to camera) - Back corners at Z=-radius Using center Z made the fog plane too small for the closer parts. Now using closestContentZ = center.z + radius to ensure the fog plane is sized for the closest part of the rotating object. This ensures corners of rotating cubes and other objects don't extend outside the fog plane boundaries when they rotate forward.

2025-09-22 05:54 UTC

2025-09-21 22:48 PDT

82da366

Clarify FOV handling in perspective projection

Added comment explaining that FOV is implicitly handled by the distance ratio calculation. When projecting from one distance to another, the angular size is preserved regardless of FOV. The perspective scale = fogPlaneDistance / contentDistance correctly handles all FOV values because it maintains angular size. All instance-specific values are properly used: - Actual fog plane Z position (this.fogPlane.position.z) - Actual camera Z position (this.camera.position.z) - Actual content Z from rotational envelope center - FOV is implicitly handled by the distance ratio

2025-09-22 05:48 UTC

2025-09-21 22:37 PDT

05acf80

Implement rotational envelope calculation for accurate bounding spheres

Added calculateRotationalEnvelope() method that finds the maximum distance from center to any vertex in the geometry. This represents the space needed for the object to rotate freely. Changes: - New method traverses all vertices and finds max distance from center - Replaces getBoundingSphere() with rotational envelope calculation - Works universally for all component types and arrangements - TextGeometry without cylinder will now show proper cylindrical bounds For TextGeometry: - Numbers at radius 0.3 will create a sphere of radius ~0.3+ - This matches the visual cylindrical form users see - Both with and without blocking cylinder will have similar bounds This ensures the bounding sphere represents what the user visually perceives, not just the minimum geometry bounds.

2025-09-22 05:37 UTC

2025-09-21 22:13 PDT

e84fa7b

Use largest bounding sphere instead of combined box for fog plane sizing

Replaced the box expansion approach (which was double-counting objects) with finding the largest bounding sphere among all objects: - Check mesh, numberGroup, individual meshes (only if not in group), and decals - Compare each object's bounding sphere - Use the largest sphere for fog plane sizing - Prevents double-counting (e.g., numbers counted individually AND in group) This approach: - More accurate for multi-object components like drum selectors - Doesn't compound bounds unnecessarily - Works for any future component with multiple objects - Should result in tighter fog plane fit For drum selector: will use the numberGroup's sphere (which contains both numbers at radius 0.3 and cylinder at 0.28 with proper width)

2025-09-22 05:13 UTC

2025-09-21 21:57 PDT

3d3e11b

Pass 2: Use instance-specific values for fog plane calculations

Implemented proper use of instance-specific values: - Added fogPlaneZ to config (default -1.5, but configurable) - Use actual fog plane Z position from this.fogPlane.position.z - Use actual camera Z position from this.camera.position.z - Camera FOV already uses this.config.cameraFOV Now each component instance can have: - Different fog plane Z positions - Different camera positions - Different FOVs And the fog plane will size correctly based on those actual values, not hardcoded assumptions. This ensures accurate fog plane sizing regardless of component configuration.

Use config.fogPlaneZ in updateFogPlaneSizeLegacy 4. Use actual fog plane position in updateFogPlaneSizeDynamic 5. b31655d

Fix perspective calculation - use closest point of bounding sphere

The fog plane was too large because we were using the sphere's center Z for perspective calculation, but content extends from center-radius to center+radius in Z direction. For a drum selector with numbers in a circle: - Some numbers are closer to camera (z = +0.3) - Some are further away (z = -0.3) - Center averages to z = 0 Using center Z made the perspective scale too large. Now using the closest point of the sphere (center.z + radius) for accurate projection. This should significantly reduce the fog plane size for drum selectors where content wraps around in Z-space.

2025-09-22 04:48 UTC

2025-09-21 21:43 PDT

6690856

Fix fog plane sizing for drum selector - include blocking cylinder in bounds

The fog plane was being calculated before the blocking cylinder was added, causing it to only account for the text numbers but not the cylinder itself. Reordered operations: 1. Create numbers 2. Create blocking cylinder 3. THEN update fog plane size This ensures the fog plane bounds include all geometry, resulting in a tighter fit around the drum selector's actual visual footprint.

2025-09-22 04:43 UTC

2025-09-21 21:39 PDT

7ed658b

Set fog plane default padding to 0

Changed default fogPlanePadding from 1.05 (5%) to 1.0 (no padding). The fog plane now fits exactly to the bounding sphere with no extra space. Padding can still be configured per component if needed.

7c1561e

Pass 2: Implement bounding sphere for rotation-invariant fog plane

Replaced bounding box with bounding sphere approach: - Use THREE.Box3.getBoundingSphere() to get rotation-invariant bounds - Sphere diameter ensures coverage at any rotation angle - Fog plane is now square (diameter x diameter) instead of rectangular - Updated console log to show sphere diameter for debugging This fixes the issue where rotating objects (like cubes viewed corner-on) would extend outside the fog plane boundaries. The bounding sphere guarantees full coverage regardless of object orientation. Example: A 1x1x1 cube has a bounding sphere diameter of ~1.73 (√3), ensuring coverage even when viewed corner-on.

48ec3b0

Pass 2: Implement fog plane perspective projection

Implemented proper perspective projection math for fog plane sizing: - Calculate center of content bounds for accurate Z position - Compute camera-to-content and camera-to-fogPlane distances - Apply perspective scale factor (fogPlane distance / content distance) - Project content bounds to fog plane Z position before applying padding - Added perspective scale to console output for debugging This fixes the issue where fog planes were too small because they were using raw geometry bounds instead of projected screen coverage. Typical perspective scale is ~1.8x for content at z=0 with fog at z=-1.5

Consider adjusting default padding after implementation The current implementation uses raw geometry bounds which are too small. Fix incorrect resize call order breaking standard 3D components

Removed erroneous resizeContainerToFitContent() call that was happening before the mesh existed. The correct flow is: 1. Create the mesh 2. THEN resize container to fit it (already handled in createMesh()) The premature resize call was causing standard 3D components to fail. This should restore all 3D components to working state.

Add TextGeometry mode to ThreeD_component_engine - the universal 3D engine

1db9382

Implement 36 dots/unit standard and universal double-sided rendering

CONE TEXTURE IMPROVEMENTS: - Updated cone texture calculations to use 36 dots/unit density standard - Base arc length formula: 2π × radius × 36 dots/unit for sector width - Slant height formula: √(height² + radius²) × 36 dots/unit for sector height - Base texture: diameter × 36 dots/unit for consistent circular resolution UNIVERSAL DOUBLE-SIDED RENDERING: - Added side: THREE.DoubleSide to ALL material creation - Cone materials: both side and base materials are double-sided - Cylinder materials: both side and cap materials are double-sided - Tube materials: already double-sided, maintained - All other geometries: single materials now double-sided by default ESTABLISHED STANDARDS: - 36 dots per unit texture density for all geometries - Double-sided rendering for all 3D engine objects - Foundation for consistent texture quality across complex shapes

df85e92

abfa9aa

d55babf

Clean up leftover generic component code and improve spacing

Three key improvements based on architecture analysis: 1. Fixed margins - Updated from 5px to 10px for better visual spacing - Slider containers: margin changed to 10px in CSS - Text input wrappers: margin changed from var(--component-spacing) to 10px 2. Removed dead CSS code - Deleted unused .component-base and .component-container classes - These were remnants from abandoned generic component approach - Added comment explaining the architectural shift 3. Refactored slider engine from inline styles to CSS - Removed inline styles from slider_component_engine.js - Now applies .slider-container class instead - Moved all styling properties to _sliders.scss - Better separation of concerns: JS = behavior, CSS = appearance This brings development branch spacing closer to main branch (which uses 20px) while cleaning up technical debt from the architectural pivot to type-specific engines. Clean up old unused code directories

The new implementation uses: - js/engines/ for all component engines - js/factory/ComponentFactory.js for component creation - Everything is now imported via ES6 modules in main.js

2025-06-09 05:18 UTC

2025-06-08 22:06 PDT

c50cd9a

Fix slider background gradients by applying theme before component initialization

The issue was that sliders were being created before the body had the data-theme attribute set. This caused the CSS selector `body[data-theme="dark"] &` to not match, so sliders would get the light theme gradient by default. Fixed by: 1. Moving theme application to the beginning of initializeSettingsComponents() 2. Checking if data-theme is already set before applying (to avoid overriding) 3. Removing duplicate savedTheme declaration This ensures the correct theme-specific gradients are applied when sliders are created.

1f6c9d9

Use percentage-based widths for responsive text input containers

- Convert pixel widths to percentages in both handleLineBreakMode and handleUnwrappedMode - Partial-width containers now use relative sizing (e.g., 60% instead of 250px) - Full-width containers use 100% instead of fixed pixel value - Should fix resize issues where containers stayed rigid during viewport changes

2025-06-05 05:48 UTC

2025-06-04 21:31 PDT

9e79f27

Fix text input height calculation during viewport resize

f1a1fc2

Revert field-sizing implementation - incompatible with wrapper architecture

2025-06-04 20:26 PDT

baf2ad2

Add progressive enhancement with CSS field-sizing

2025-06-04 19:46 PDT

287246f

Remove adjustHeight override that was sabotaging mode logic

147d0e3

d91e067

Fix: Increase resize debounce timeout to prevent clipping

- Changed timeout from 50ms to 200ms in handleSizeApproximation - Prevents text input from clipping at ~13 lines during viewport resize - ResizeObserver events during active resize typically come 60-100ms apart - 200ms timeout ensures recalculation only happens after resize completes - Eliminates incorrect container width readings during active resize

53f41a7

Fix select-all-delete not resizing in wrapped mode

2025-06-03 01:38 UTC

0820e3c

Implement state-aware sizing for text inputs to fix wrapped mode jumping

- Added wrapped vs unwrapped state detection (95% of container width) - Wrapped mode: Keep width at max, only adjust height based on scrollHeight - Unwrapped mode: Use existing approximation for both width and height - Eliminates jumping behavior when typing short text on last line of wrapped inputs - Viewport resizes still trigger full recalculation with forceApproximation flag - Console logging added to debug wrapped/unwrapped mode behavior

2025-06-03 01:11 UTC

2025-06-02 17:50 PDT

9fab1c2

Fix text input jump behavior - remove 50-character threshold

- Always use smooth approximation for all text lengths - Removes jumpy behavior for normal typing (under 50 chars) - Maintains hybrid approach with refinement after 50ms - Provides consistent smooth experience regardless of text length - Fixes issue where text like "jkj kj kj kjk kkj kjk jkj kjk kkk" would jump with each character

2025-06-03 00:50 UTC

2025-06-02 15:48 PDT

c8abdf9

Apply safe padding to horizontal only

- Changed from padding: var(--input-safe-padding) (all sides) - To padding: 0 var(--input-safe-padding) (horizontal only) - Removes unnecessary vertical padding - Relies on font's natural line-height for vertical spacing - Should reduce visual padding by ~50%

2025-06-02 22:48 UTC

2025-06-02 14:11 PDT

8755984

Reduce safe padding to absolute minimum

- Simplified formula to just: calc(var(--input-border-radius) * 0.3) - Removed redundant 0.2em base padding - Relies on font's natural line-height for vertical spacing - Results in minimal 6px padding for 20px radius - Prevents clipping without excess spacing

2025-06-02 21:11 UTC

2025-06-02 12:36 PDT

60926be

Implement dynamic safe padding architecture for input components

- Added CSS variables to root: --input-border-radius and --input-safe-padding - Safe padding formula: calc(0.2em + var(--input-border-radius) * 0.3) - Padding scales with both font size (em) and border radius - Removed overflow:hidden - no longer needed as padding prevents clipping - Mathematical relationship ensures text never enters rounded corner zones - Central control: change border radius once, padding adjusts everywhere

2025-06-02 19:36 UTC

f4d274a

d84d023

Extend hybrid sizing to all text input scenarios

d839b40

Implement hybrid paste sizing for instant text input response

- Add handlePasteApproximation() for large pastes (50+ chars) - Measure text as single line to calculate approximate dimensions - If fits in container: use exact width - If doesn't fit: calculate rows needed + 1 buffer - Apply dimensions instantly without transitions - Schedule precise refinement after 50ms - Removes slow growth feedback loop on paste - Also removed temporary transition test code

2025-05-26 05:25 UTC

2025-05-25 21:54 PDT

9a60002

Fix CSS styling issues - import CSS files into webpack bundle

Root cause: The component engines generate HTML but expect CSS to be loaded separately. In the main branch, CSS files were dynamically loaded by selector-init.js when the settings page loaded. Our webpack bundle didn't include this mechanism. Solution: 1. Import the three critical CSS files directly in main.js: - styles/slider-buttons.css (slider-specific styles) - js/core/slider-styles.css (unified slider styles) - js/inputs/input-styles.css (input component styles) 2. Install css-loader and style-loader for webpack 3. Configure webpack to handle CSS files 4. Rebuild bundle (now 172 KiB, up from 154 KiB) This should restore: - Pill-shaped slider backgrounds with gradients - Proper navbar padding containers - Professional appearance matching main branch - All component visual styles

2025-05-26 04:54 UTC

2025-05-25 21:24 PDT

895627a

Fix SPA script execution issue - move settings initialization to main.js

The issue was that scripts inside HTML loaded via innerHTML don't execute, which is a browser security feature. This prevented initializeSettingsComponents from being defined when the settings page was loaded via the router. Solution: Move the settings component initialization function from the settings page HTML into main.js where it can be called directly by the router after page load. This ensures components will render properly when navigating to the settings page in the SPA architecture.

500932c

feat: Complete Phase 1 component architecture implementation

- Add GenericSelector component extending existing SelectorBase - Configurable options, labels, and callbacks - Integrates with SliderCore for smooth animations - Supports theme and time format selectors - Add TextInput component extending existing InputBase - Built-in validation system (email, phone, length limits) - Real-time validation feedback - Custom event dispatch for integration - Add ComponentFactory for centralized component management - Registration system for selectors and inputs - Bulk initialization with error handling - Instance management and debugging utilities - Configure all main branch element replacements - Theme selector: Dark/System/Light with proper integration - Time format: 24h/System/12h with event dispatch - Text inputs: firstName, lastName, nickname with validation - Fix ID mismatch: Updated to kebab-case (first-name, last-name) - Add comprehensive testing infrastructure - Individual component test suites - Integration test with 15+ test cases - Debug utilities and visual interfaces - SPA navigation testing - Preserve identical UX while adding enhanced functionality - Matches main branch CSS classes exactly - Maintains all animations and transitions - Same localStorage keys and persistence - Fallback behavior for error recovery All 8 steps of Phase 1 complete. Universal components ready to replace hardcoded main branch elements with zero visual regression.

2025-05-15 07:04 UTC

2025-05-14 20:08 PDT

ff5f6f7

Restore improved component architecture

- Add ComponentBase abstract class with full lifecycle management - Add ComponentUtils with helper functions - Add SelectorBase for selector-specific functionality - Add GenericSelector implementation - Add user settings configuration system - Add unified component styling - Update generic-selector.js with improved architecture

lkjlkjlkjl

Removed all inline onclick handlers from the HTML and replaced them with proper event listeners in JavaScript Added data-theme attributes to the options for clarity Improved the event handler setup in theme-selector.js with a dedicated setupOptionClickHandlers() function Updated theme-integration.js to work with the new data-theme attributes Enhanced the router.js file to properly load theme resources before rendering the page content Added localStorage saving directly in the setActiveOption function for better encapsulation
